name: Montserrat (United Kingdom)

colors.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign
the saltire and cross of the Union Flag canton; Erin's robe in the coat of arms
the cross of St George and saltire of St Patrick in the Union Flag canton
the field of the Montserrat coat of arms (the 'Emerald Isle of the Caribbean')
the harp held by Erin in the coat of arms
the cross and the figure of Erin

description: A British Blue Ensign — navy field with the Union Flag in the upper-hoist canton — defaced in the fly with the coat of arms of Montserrat: a woman in white (Erin / Hibernia) embracing a dark cross and holding a golden harp, on a green shield.
history: Adopted on 10 April 1909 with subsequent minor revisions. Montserrat's distinctive coat of arms reflects its settlement by Irish Catholics from the early seventeenth century onwards.

features.symbolism: the field of the British Blue Ensign, denoting Montserrat's status as a British Overseas Territory
Montserrat's status as a British Overseas Territory

Erin (the female personification of Ireland) holding a harp and embracing a Christian cross — a reference to the strong Irish heritage of Montserrat, which earned it the nickname 'Emerald Isle of the Caribbean'
